title: How The Fox Factory (FOXF) Investment Story Is Shifting As Price Targets Tighten
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

Fox Factory Holding now sits in a price target range clustered between US$19 and US$26, with a modest fair value update from US$22.57 to US$22.83 per share. That spread reflects differing analyst views on how effectively the company can respond to softer trends in Bike and Marucci, while tightening costs and focusing on cash generation. As you read on, you will see how these shifting targets feed into the evolving narrative and what to watch if you want to stay in step with future revisions.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value Fox Factory Holding.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Stifel lifted its price target to US$26 from US$25 and kept a Buy rating. This places the firm at the upper end of the current US$19 to US$26 range you are seeing across the Street. The higher Stifel target indicates confidence that Fox Factory's focus on cost control and cash generation can support value at levels above the more cautious scenarios being discussed.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Roth Capital cut its price target to US$19 from US$21 and maintained a Neutral rating, citing weakness in the Bike segment and a downturn at Marucci after recent meetings with management. Roth highlights a period of adjustment, with management working to align costs with more realistic growth goals and emphasizing free cash flow and debt repayment rather than aggressive expansion.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!NasdaqGS:FOXF 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 1 risk for Fox Factory Holding. See which could impact your investment.

What's in the News

Fox Factory issued first quarter 2026 net sales guidance of US$343m to US$369m, giving you a clearer sense of near term revenue expectations. For full year 2026, the company guided to net sales between US$1.328b and US$1.416b, outlining the revenue range management is planning around. Fox Factory reported impairment charges for the three months ended January 2, 2026, including US$295,178,000 of goodwill impairment and US$13,517,000 of intangible and long lived asset impairment, which directly affects reported earnings and book value. The company entered a cooperation agreement with Engine Capital Management that adds two new independent directors and creates a Board level Transformation Committee focused on operational performance and profitability.

Story Continues

How This Changes the Fair Value For Fox Factory Holding

Fair value updated from US$22.57 to US$22.83 per share. Revenue growth assumption revised from 3.46% growth to a 2.26% decline. Net profit margin adjusted from 4.68% to 10.37%. Future P/E reduced from 16.98x to 9.22x. Discount rate moved from 10.64% to 10.92%.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives link a company's business story to the assumptions behind its forecasts and fair value, so you can see how product, market, and risk trends fit together. They refresh as new data and research come through, keeping the thesis current.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on Fox Factory Holding to stay up to date on:

How premium product launches in areas like e-bikes, entry premium bikes, and connected suspension are expected to support demand and margins. What expanding into EVs, hybrid vehicles, motorcycles, and new geographies such as Japan for Marucci could mean for Fox Factory's long term growth drivers. Key risks around tariffs, concentrated manufacturing, consumer cyclical exposure, and rising operating costs that could pressure margins and earnings if conditions do not improve.
